topic: real estate showing tips — what he learned at brokerage training

turns.content: bro they had us doing mock showings today at training and i was lowkey nervous lol
Ha, that's totally normal! Mock showings can feel awkward at first. How did it go? What kind of scenario did they have you run through?
they gave us a fake listing sheet and we had to walk another trainee through it like they were a buyer. i kept forgetting to point out stuff like the water heater age and the electrical panel

Those are exactly the kinds of details that come with practice. The good news is you're catching what you missed now, in training, rather than in front of an actual client. Did the instructor give you any tips on how to remember all those checklist items?
yeah she said start from the outside and work your way in. like roof condition, gutters, foundation cracks, then go room by room. make it a routine so u dont skip anything

That's a solid approach — outside-in, systematic. It becomes muscle memory after a while. Kind of like a pilot's pre-flight checklist. Did they cover how to handle tough buyer questions, like if someone asks about stuff you don't know?
yeah bro they said never make stuff up. just say let me find out for you and get back to them. honestly thats how i am anyway i dont bs people

That's a great quality to have in this business. Clients can tell when someone's being straight with them versus making things up. Honesty builds trust, and trust gets you referrals. Are you feeling more confident about doing actual showings now?
yeah a little bit. one thing she also said is to always let the buyer walk into the room first. like dont crowd them. let them feel the space on their own

That's a great tip — giving buyers space to envision themselves in the home without you hovering. It lets them form their own emotional connection to the property. People buy based on how a place makes them feel, not just the specs on paper.

facts bro. she said the best agents talk less and listen more. like read the buyer and figure out what they actually care about. some people care about the kitchen some people care about the yard. just pay attention

Exactly — active listening is a superpower in sales. Once you know what someone values most, you can highlight the right features without overwhelming them. That kind of awareness separates good agents from great ones.
inshallah bro. i just gotta get reps in. like anything else u know. driving was the same way first week i was lost now i know every street in houston

Exactly — that driving experience is actually a huge advantage. You already know the neighborhoods, the traffic patterns, where things are. A lot of new agents don't have that kind of local knowledge. You're going to crush it once you get those reps in.
wallahi thats what the trainer said too. she was like your city knowledge is an asset. made me feel good ngl. aight bro gotta sleep early training again tomorrow
Get that rest! Sounds like the training is going well and you're picking things up fast. Good luck tomorrow — keep building those reps. You've got this.
